The design conflates error reporting with token stream, and the output class does not redirect to error.txt when errors are present.]

88.reason: Operator precedence: `||` has lower precedence than `&&`, so the condition is `(sign2 == 1 && input.charAt(pos)!='*') || (sign1 ==1)`. This causes the loop to run whenever sign1 is 1, even if sign2 is 0, and the loop body does not check for `*` to exit; it only increments pos. This can lead to an infinite loop or skipping characters incorrectly, especially when sign1 is set from a previous line.

This causes the loop to run whenever sign1 is 1, even if sign2 is 0, and the loop body does not check for `*` to exit; it only increments pos. This can lead to an infinite loop or skipping characters incorrectly, especially when sign1 is set from a previous line.

384.reason: The problem requires that for erroneous programs, the output should be written to "error.txt", but here it is always writing to "lexer.txt". When sign==1 (indicating an error), the file should be "error.txt".
